reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly in Content Accuracy and instruction following. Instead of performing the required paralinguistic feature (a scream) as a non-linguistic vocalization or tonal shift, the model reads the instruction word 'Scream' aloud as part of the sentence at 00:00. This is a hallucination of the prompt instructions into the speech content. Consequently, the intended emotional expression is lost because the model interprets the style command as text to be spoken.
reasoning_model_2: The model correctly separates the instruction from the content. It produces the script 'Ah! There is a mouse there' starting at 00:00 without reading the prompt instructions. By successfully integrating the exclamation 'Ah!' into the speech flow, it attempts the required vocalization context. The content is accurate, and the delivery follows the script structure without the critical error observed in Model 1.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 commits a critical error by reading the prompt instruction 'Scream' as part of the spoken text (00:00), which violates Content Accuracy and fails to implement the feature as a paralinguistic modification. Model 2 correctly interprets the text and avoids hallucinating instructions. While the intensity of Model 2's scream cannot be fully verified as perfect without audio, it is functionally superior because it actually attempts to perform the script rather than reading the meta-data.
